warnings: Per operator correction dated 2026-07-11, the 4 center .531 DR. THRU holes are NOT symmetric and have been extracted as 4 individually-positioned hole features (F006-F009) rather than a single symmetric pattern callout. However the drawing's dimensioning (6.781, .500, 7.000, 6.000, 6.500) visually suggests a near-symmetric layout; the exact individual offsets for holes 2-4 were inferred from the visible spacing dims (.500 and 7.000) combined with baseline 6.781, and may need field verification.

The bottom-right corner counterbore hole (H004/F005) position was inferred from the 14.00 vertical envelope and 16.656 horizontal position (mirrored from top-right hole) since no direct dimension explicitly locates it independently from the top-right hole other than the 14.00 total span - flagged for verification.

Y-origin assumed at bottom edge of plate (0,0) per instructions; hole Y-offsets computed by subtracting from top edge (11.25) using dimensions D004, D007, D008, D009 - arithmetic chain should be verified against original drawing since some values were reconstructed by combining multiple dimensions.
Drawing is hand-sketched with some numbers written in pencil/cursive; several dimensions (esp. 6.250 and 14.00 relationship) are somewhat ambiguous in exact witness-line association.
No explicit chamfer or fillet callouts were found on this drawing; none were added.
No FRONT view found — the base profile cannot be built without it.

overview_analysis.overall_shape_summary: A flat rectangular steel plate, 17.50 x 11.250 in overall and 1.00 in thick, with four corner counterbored clearance holes for .500 socket head cap screws and four through-drilled .531 dia holes arranged in an inner rectangular pattern.

views_detected.description: Top (plan) view of the rectangular plate showing overall length/width, corner counterbore holes, and inner .531 dia through-hole pattern with X/Y positional dimensions.
Thin side/edge (profile) view showing the plate thickness (1.00 in) and hidden lines indicating the depth/step of the counterbore feature.
Title block with material (1018 STL), heat treatment, drawn/checked/approved dates, model number 250 BX, drawing number 127-C, and general notes on tolerances and projection.

symmetry.notes: Both the 4 corner counterbore holes and 4 inner through-holes are located using explicit X/Y linear offset dimensions (e.g., .844, .750, 15.812, 11.250, 6.781, 7.000, 6.000, 6.500, 6.250, 14.00) rather than polar/angular callouts, which is evidence against assuming a true rotational bolt-circle pattern. The overall rectangular outline and hole placement appear visually symmetric about the plate's center, but this should be treated as coincidental rectangular layout rather than a specified symmetric pattern.

flags_contributed.human_note: CROSS-VIEW CONFLICT (top, side): The side view is drawn very thin/schematic and does not clearly distinguish the counterbore depth from the through-hole depth for all 8 holes; only two hidden-line hole representations are shown, making it ambiguous which corresponds to which of the two hole patterns (counterbore vs. thru-drill) at each of the 8 locations. Recommendation: Verify counterbore depth and thru-hole condition against a section view or additional callout; if none exists, confirm with the checker (JWZ) that side view hidden lines represent both hole types correctly.

CROSS-VIEW CONFLICT (top): Corner hole pattern dimensions (.844, .750, 15.812, 11.250) and inner hole pattern dimensions (6.781, 7.000, .500, 6.000, 6.500, 6.250, 14.00) are both given in linear X/Y offsets from what appear to be different reference edges/datums, with no explicit common datum flagged; ensure no double-dimensioning conflict exists between the 17.50/15.812 and 14.00/6.250 chains. Recommendation: Cross-check that the 15.812 and 17.50 dimensions to the corner holes, and the 14.00/6.250 to the inner holes, reference the same left/bottom edges consistently; confirm no redundant or conflicting datum definition exists.

HOLE-COUNT DISAGREEMENT: the sheet's global callout "UNLESS OTHERWISE SPECIFIED: THIRD ANGLE PROJECTION, DIMENSIONS IN INCHES, 3 PLACE DIM. ±.010, 2 PLACE DIM. ±.02, ANGULAR DIM. ±, SCALE 1"=2"" states 3 instance(s), but the per-view extraction captured hole group(s) of [1, 1, 1, 1, 4] (total 8). A cropped view cannot resolve this alone — check for an occluded instance (behind the title block or a leader line) before building, or the part will silently miss a feature.

resolution.plain_english: All dimensions confirmed by arithmetic chain or explicit callout. Model is complete and buildable as-is.

constraints.source_text: THe 4 holes that failed are not symmetircal they need to be featured individually as seperate sketched one at a time in their designated locatiions
constraints.notes: 4 holes must be modeled individually as separate sketches, one at a time, at their designated locations; not a symmetrical/circular pattern
